titleStmt.title: Letter from M. J. Heverin to the under-secretary, 3 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: M. J. Heverin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from M.J. Heverin, Secretary, Roscommon County Council to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), the under-secretary for ireland. Chalmers succeeded Sir Matthew Nathan who resigned on 3 May, 1916 due to his failure to prevent the Easter Rising.
Mr. Heverin encloses a resolution passed unanimously by Roscommon County Council. The resolution refers to previous difficulties in relation to uneven distribution of land in the county which led to the deployment of large numbers of military and police to the area. As these matters have been settled the Council calls for the withdrawal of these forces.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5620/13368

correspAction.placeName: Roscommon County Council, Courthouse, Roscommon, County Roscommon, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Roscommon County Council
SECRETARY'S OFFICE,
COURTHOUSE.
ROSCOMMON 3rd August 1916 encl. Sir/ I herewith enclose copy resolution which was
passed unanimously by the Roscommon County Council
at their Meeting on the 1st instant. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, M J Heverin The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle. Asst. Under Secretary
Submitted to see
on return the resolution will
be ackd.
W.O.H.
4/8/16 Chief Secretary
U.S. to see Resolution,Co Council
Eof
4.8.16
ROSCOMMON COUNTY COUNCIL RESOLUTION Proposed by Councillor Patrick Murray, Seconded by Councillor M. McDonnell, And Resolved — "That we call the attention of the
Government to the fact that the discontent of the
people of the Parish of Moore which recently found
expression 1 was due to the want of economic farms on
the part of hundreds of small farmers in that part of
the county whilst one man possesses 3,500 acres of land
in the Union of Ballinasloe. The late agitation for
meadow land for the present season has ceased owing to
a settlement, but a large force of military and police
is still retained there unecessarily, and this martial
law is a source of irritation to the local people, and
we request the Government to immediately remove them.
1. By injuring 17 RIC, one seriously.
RIC 4/8/16 At the Meeting of the County Council held this 1st day of August 1916
the above Resolution was (a) Carried
Signature of Presiding Chairman M. H. Grogan. (a) Insert here "Carried" or "Lost" ; if on a Division add the word "by a majority of ...........
votes against............... votes," the names of those voting being—
